title: The 7 Best Herbal Essential Oils for Cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for cough because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the throat, making it easier to breathe.

Α-pinene, commonly known as ginger, is good for a cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
Eucalyptol is good for cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Limonene is good for cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat and airways.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil is a popular remedy for coughs. This oil is made from the leaves of the Eucalyptus tree, which is native to Australia. The leaves of this tree contain a compound called eucalyptol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. When inhaled, eucalyptol helps to open up airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. This is especially helpful for people with coughs caused by congestion, such as colds and flu. Eucalyptus globulus essential oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease discomfort. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to relieve stuffiness and congestion, making it easier to cough up mucus. When used in aromatherapy, Eucalyptus globulus essential oil can help to calm a cough and provide relief from congestion, making it a natural and effective remedy for coughs.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale, also known as Ginger, herbal essential oil that helps with cough, you will need fresh ginger roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a glass jar, and a clean environment. First, peel the fresh ginger roots and chop them into small pieces. Next, combine the chopped ginger with a carrier oil in a glass jar, making sure the ginger pieces are fully covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This process is called maceration, which helps to release the active compounds from the ginger into the carrier oil. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the ginger solids. The resulting liquid is the Ginger herbal essential oil, which can be used to help soothe a cough. To use it, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil or water and rub it on the chest or inhale it through steam inhalation.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for cough because it helps to thin mucus and temporarily relieve congestion, making it easier to cough up and feel better.
Menthone, commonly known as ginger, is good for cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, reducing inflammation and providing relief from congestion and irritation.
Rosmarinic acid, also known as turmeric, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, which can help alleviate cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oil, also known as peppermint oil, has been used for centuries to help soothe a cough. This is because peppermint oil contains compounds like menthol and menthone, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, these compounds can help thin mucus in the throat and lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion. Additionally, peppermint oil has a calming effect on the body, which can help to reduce irritation and discomfort in the throat and chest. This can be especially helpful for people with a sore or scratchy throat due to a cough or cold. Furthermore, peppermint oil can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of coughs by relieving digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, which can be triggers for coughing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils that help with cough, you will need fresh ginger roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, peel and chop the fresh ginger roots into small pieces, then place them in the glass jar. Next, pour the carrier oil over the ginger pieces, making sure they are completely covered. Now, place the jar in a warm, dark place, such as a cupboard, and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called infusion, and it allows the active compounds in the ginger to dissolve into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Zingiber officinale herbal essential oil is now ready to use, and you can apply it topically to help soothe a cough or add it to a diffuser for inhalation.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for cough because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause coughing.

Carvacrol, commonly known as ginger, is good for cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid, which is commonly known as ginger, is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oil, also known as thyme oil, is a popular remedy for coughs. This oil is extracted from the leaves of the thyme plant, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The main reason thyme oil is effective for coughs is its antimicrobial properties. It contains compounds like thymol, which helps to kill bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause respiratory infections. When inhaled, thyme oil can help to clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme oil has a calming effect on the body, which can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing. It can be used in a diffuser, added to a warm bath, or mixed with a carrier oil for a massage to help relieve a cough. Thyme oil is also a natural expectorant, which means it can help to bring up mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to expel.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale, or ginger, herbal essential oil, you will need fresh ginger roots, a carrier oil, and a clean glass jar. First, peel the ginger roots to remove the skin, then chop them into small pieces. Next, fill a clean glass jar with the chopped ginger and cover it with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. The ratio of ginger to carrier oil should be about 10-15% ginger to 85-90% carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. During this time, the ginger will infuse its properties into the carrier oil. After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This ginger essential oil can be used to help relieve coughs, as ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion. However, it's essential to note that this homemade oil is not as concentrated as commercial essential oils, so use it in moderation and dilute it further with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin or using it in aromatherapy.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Iridoids, also known as Ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity of cough and promoting relaxation.
Polyphenols, also known as Ginger, help to soothe a cough by reducing inflammation and relaxing the airways, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable.
Flavonoids, commonly known as ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the swelling and irritation in the throat, making it an effective natural remedy for cough relief.

Tannins, also known as Ginger, are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oil is often used to help with coughs due to its anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, the oil can help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections that may be causing the cough. The oil can also help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. This can be especially helpful for people with coughs caused by colds or flu, as it can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils, also known as ginger essential oil, which helps with cough, you'll need fresh ginger roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, peel and chop the fresh ginger into small pieces, making sure to remove any bruised or damaged parts. Next, fill a clean glass jar about 1/4 full with the chopped ginger and cover it with a carrier oil, making sure that the ginger is completely submerged. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the oil extract from the ginger. After the extraction period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the ginger solids. The resulting ginger essential oil can be used to help soothe a cough by adding a few drops to a warm bath, inhaling it directly, or mixing it with other herbs to create a cough-relieving remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil, also known as rosemary oil, has several benefits for a cough. The oil contains compounds that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. This makes it easier to breathe and can help to reduce congestion. Rosemary oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an irritated throat and reduce swelling. Additionally, its decongestant properties can help to open up airways, allowing for better airflow and reducing coughing. The oil's natural expectorant properties can also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, which can provide relief from a persistent cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal essential oil, also known as ginger essential oil, which helps with cough, you will need fresh ginger roots, a carrier oil, and a clean glass jar. First, peel and chop the ginger roots into small pieces, then place them in the glass jar. Next, pour carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, over the ginger pieces to completely cover them. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will allow the ginger to infuse its healing properties into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the ginger pieces. Your ginger essential oil is now ready to use, but be sure to dilute it with another carrier oil before applying it to your skin, as it can be quite strong. You can use this oil to make a cough syrup by mixing it with honey and water, or add a few drops to your diffuser to help relieve a cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, ease congestion, and calm the cough, making it easier to breathe and swallow.

Flavonoids, also known as ginger, are good for cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and clear out mucus.

Polyphenols, commonly known as ginger, are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has been used for centuries to treat coughs and other respiratory issues. Its essential oil is extracted from the root of the plant and contains compounds that help soothe and calm the throat. When inhaled, the oil can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing spasms. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm the stomach and reduce acid reflux, which can trigger or worsen coughs. Additionally, the oil contains expectorants that help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear the airways. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil a popular natural remedy for treating coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale herbal essential oil for cough relief, start by harvesting fresh ginger roots, making sure they are clean and free of any dirt. Next, dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator on a low temperature. Once the ginger is dry, peel the roots to remove the outer layer, and then chop them into small pieces. After that, use a steam distillation process or a solvent-based extraction method to extract the essential oil from the ginger. Steam distillation involves heating the ginger pieces in a sealed container with water and then collecting the oil that rises into the air. This oil can be collected and used as is, or further refined to create a higher-quality product. The resulting Zingiber officinale essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe a cough when inhaled or applied topically, making it a natural remedy for cough relief.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve coughing.

Shogaols, also known as ginger, are good for cough because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cough.
Zingiberene is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.
Curcumenol is good for cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, has been used for centuries to help soothe coughs and colds. One of the reasons it's good for cough is that it has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When we have a cough, it's often because our airways are inflamed, which makes it hard to breathe. Ginger oil can help reduce this inflammation and make it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger oil has antibacterial properties, which can help fight off infections that can cause coughs. The oil also contains compounds that help thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up. This can help relieve congestion and make it easier to breathe. Furthermore, ginger oil has a soothing effect on the throat, which can help calm a cough and provide relief from discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Zingiber officinale (ginger) herbal essential oil to help with cough, start by harvesting fresh ginger roots. Wash and clean the roots thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities. Dry the roots in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on a low temperature setting to prevent any damage. Once the roots are dry, peel and chop them into small pieces. In a clean glass jar, combine 1 part of the dried ginger with 2 parts of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Seal the jar and leave it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day to allow the ginger to infuse its properties into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your ginger essential oil is now ready to use as a natural remedy for coughs and sore throats.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for cough

intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great help for a cough. There are two common herbs used for this - Eucalyptus globulus and Mentha x piperita, also known as Peppermint. Eucalyptus globulus helps to open up the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the discomfort of a cough. It has a decongestant effect, which means it helps to remove excess mucus from the lungs, giving you more relief. Mentha x piperita, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe the throat and reduce the severity of the cough. When you have a cough, it's not just a minor annoyance - it can keep you awake at night, make it hard to work or enjoy activities, and even lead to more serious health problems like bronchitis or pneumonia. By using herbal essential oils, you can help to manage your cough and get back to your normal life, where you can enjoy activities, spend time with loved ones, and get a good night's sleep.
supplementary_best: A combination of 'Eucalyptus globulus', 'Mentha x piperita', and 'Thymus vulgaris' essential oils can help alleviate a cough. Eucalyptus globulus oil has decongestant properties that help clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. 'Mentha x piperita' oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the throat and reduce coughing. 'Thymus vulgaris' oil, also known as thyme oil, contains compounds that have antiseptic properties, helping to fight off infections that can cause a cough. This combination of oils can be used in aromatherapy, added to a humidifier or diffuser, or mixed with a carrier oil for topical application. When applied correctly, this blend can help ease a cough and promote respiratory health.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a cough, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can irritate your throat or lungs further. 'Cassia' oil, for example, is very strong and can be too harsh for people with respiratory issues. This oil can cause more irritation and discomfort. 'Clove' oil is another oil to avoid, as it has a strong, pungent smell that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, 'Wintergreen' oil contains salicylic acid, which can be problematic for people with certain health conditions or who are taking medications. 'Nutmeg' oil is also not recommended, as it can be toxic in large quantities and may cause more harm than good. 'Sassafras' oil is another oil to steer clear of, as it has been linked to potential health issues, including respiratory problems.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. It has thick, sticky properties that can calm an irritated throat and reduce inflammation. When you swallow, honey coats the throat, making it less likely to get inflamed and irritated. This makes it a good option for coughs caused by colds and flu. Slurping on warm honey tea can help loosen mucus and reduce coughing.  Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat, which can cause coughing. You can make ginger tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water, then straining it before drinking. Ginger can help ease coughing by reducing inflammation and soothing the throat.  Slippery elm is a natural demulcent that forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes in the throat, helping to soothe and calm irritation. It can help reduce inflammation and ease coughing, making it a good option for coughs caused by colds and flu. You can take slippery elm as a supplement or make a tea by steeping dried slippery elm bark in hot water.  Slurping on warm chicken soup can help ease a cough by loosening mucus and providing fluids to the body. The steam from the soup can also help ease congestion in the sinuses and throat, making it easier to breathe.  Salt water gargling can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the throat, which can cause coughing. When you gargle with salt water, the salt helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, which can help ease coughing.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have a cough may also experience other health issues. Bronchitis is a common problem, where the airways in the lungs become inflamed. The herb thyme is often used to help with bronchitis because it has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the infection that's causing the cough. Thyme also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling in the airways.  Another issue that people with a cough may face is pneumonia, a serious infection that inflames the air sacs in the lungs. The herb echinacea is sometimes used to help prevent pneumonia, as it has immune-boosting properties that can help the body fight off the infection. Echinacea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling in the lungs.  Some people with a cough may also experience sinus infections, where the sinuses become infected and inflamed. The herb eucalyptus is often used to help with sinus infections because it has decongestant properties, which can help clear out mucus and reduce congestion in the sinuses. Eucalyptus also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the infection that's causing the sinus infection.  People with a cough may also experience asthma, a condition where the airways become inflamed and constricted. The herb licorice root is sometimes used to help with asthma because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling in the airways. Licorice root also has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways.  Lastly, people with a cough may also experience a sore throat, which can be caused by the constant coughing. The herb slippery elm is often used to help with a sore throat because it has soothing properties that can help calm the throat and reduce inflammation. Slippery elm also has demulcent properties, which can help form a protective barrier over the throat to help it heal.
